ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08593Timothy Leary Manuscripts and Ephemera8593Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
December 20172B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
physcThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:11:56 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Timothy Leary Manuscripts and Ephemera
McWhinnie, John
8593
.6 cubic feet. .6 cubic feet.
1940-1986. Correspondence, manuscripts. The materials by and relating to Leary in this collection were assembled by rare books and contemporary art dealer John McWhinnie. This collection contains circa 150 items comprised of manuscripts and documents by Leary; letters to and from Leary, including additional correspondence concerning him; documents and manuscripts relating to Leary's colleagues and associates in the psychedelic movement, and the various institutions and organizations they created; and psychedelic-related ephemera. Collection material in English 9898423
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
December 2017
EAD encoding:
Jude Corina & Lin Sen Chai, December 2017
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Timothy Leary (1920-1996) was an American psychologist and writer known for advocating the exploration of the therapeutic potential of psychedelic drugs.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
The materials by and relating to Leary in this collection were assembled by rare books and contemporary art dealer John McWhinnie.
This collection contains circa 150 items comprised of manuscripts and documents by Leary; letters to and from Leary, including additional correspondence concerning him; documents and manuscripts relating to Leary's colleagues and associates in the psychedelic movement, and the various institutions and organizations they created; and psychedelic-related ephemera.
Cite As:
Timothy Leary Manuscripts and Ephemera, #8593. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Psychotropic drugs Psychotropic drugs. Drugs. Leary, Timothy, 1920-1996.
9898423 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I: Leary manuscripts and documents1940-1950Typescript photocopy, Declaration of Independence for Dr. Timothy Leary. July 4, 1971 (Draft)Leary, Timothy1971-07-0411
Scope and Contents
Statement defending Leary, with autograph note: "Allen Ginsberg arranged! / [sketch of smiling heart] / "Timothy Leary / 9-19-95." Draft text for pamphlet of the same title.Declaration of Independence for Dr. Timothy Leary. July 4, 1971Leary, Timothy1971-07-0412The Interpersonal Dimension of PersonalityLeary, TimothyFreedman, Mervin1951-1213
Scope and Contents
Reprint from Journal of Personality. Vol. 20, no. 2. December, 1951. Pages 143-161Psychedelic Prayers after the tao te chingLeary, Timothy196614
Scope and Contents
New Hyde Park, N.Y.: University Books. n.d.Summary of the Dissertation Submitted in Partial Satisfaction of the Requirements for the Degree of Doctor of PhilosophyLeary, Timothy Francis1950-1215
Scope and Contents
University of California, Graduate Division, Northern Section.The Neo-American Church Catechism and Handbook: A Review by Timothy LearyLeary, Timothy196716A Constructive Proposal to End the LSD CrisisLeary, TimothyUndated17Religious Implications of Consciousness Expanding SubstancesLeary, TimothyClark, Walter Houston1963-05-1963-0618
Scope and Contents
Reprinted from Religious EducationDMT ReportLeary, Timothy1962-12-1019
Scope and Contents
Description of personal DMT experienceSession #16 - Zi. Subject #1Leary, TimothyUndated110Untitled mimeographed typescriptLeary, Timothy1962-11111
Scope and Contents
Summary of LSD research at Harvard for a Los Angles audienceStarseed Bulletin; Update: July 1974-February 1975Leary, Timothy1975112
Scope and Contents
An addendum describing Leary's recent activities since the publication "of this book" (presumably Starseed).Partly printed check signed, ("Timothy Leary")Leary, Timothy1955-12-01113
Scope and Contents
Check for five dollars payable to "NAACP," on American Trust Company account, Oakland, CA. Cancellation stamps.School and Society, Volume 64Leary, Timothy1946-07-20114
Scope and Contents
Contains Leary's essay, "Progress Toward Solving the Problem of Race Relations," (pp. 41-43).Partial Typescript: First LSD ExperimentLeary, Timothy1962115
Scope and Contents
A typed description by Leary, heavily annotated, of his first meeting with Huxley, his initial purchase of LSD, and the group they gathered to conduct the first experiment (which included Huxley)Typescript Commodore Leri StoryLeary, TimothyUndated116"LSD - Tepoztlan"Leary, Timothy1962-06117
Scope and Contents
Leary's description of taking LSD in Mexico and engaging in a threesomeLeague for Spiritual DiscoveryLeary, Timothy1966118
Scope and Contents
Description and purposes of the League for Spiritual Discovery, "a non-profit religious association" which advocated the use of LSD. Leary listed as first member of "Board of Guides" (p.5).United States Corps of Cadets Card, issued to LearyLeary, Timothy1940119The Religious Experience: Its Production and InterpretationLeary, Timothy1970120Typescript of "revision of the article on drugs," for Esquire magazineLeary, Timothy1964-03-12121
Scope and Contents
With attached carbon of March 12, 1964 letter (unsigned) from Leary to Harold Hayes, editor of Esquire, resubmitting the manuscript; typewritten corrections throughoutReactions To Psilocybin Administered in a Supportive Environment.Leary, TimothyLitwin, George H.Metzner, Ralph1963-12122
Scope and Contents
Reprint from The Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease, Vol. 137, No. 6, December 1963Statement by Timothy LearyLeary, Timothy1971123
Scope and Contents
Leary justifies his plea of not guilty to federal drug charges.Americans and Mushrooms in a Naturalistic Environment: A Preliminary ReportLeary, TimothyLitwin, George H.Metzner, Ralph1962124Annotated Partial Transcript of InterviewLeary, Timothy1975125
Scope and Contents
Three original typescript pages from a longer interview of Leary, which has been heavily edited and emended in autograph, possibly by him.Genetic Roulette Circuit 7Leary, Timothy1972-12126
Scope and Contents
Original typescript"One night in the winter I crossed the Rockies…"Leary, TimothyUndated127
Scope and Contents
Original typescript story featuring Little Richard, Fatty Arbuckle, Floyd Patterson, and talking goats.Draft annotated typescript, "HIP — new lead."Leary, TimothyUndated128
Scope and Contents
Original typescript of article describing a San Francisco peace rally.A New Behavior Change Program Using PsilocybinLeary, TimothyMetzner, RalphWeil, GuntherSchwitzgebel, RalphKinne, Sara1965129Typescript essay, untitled. ("A journalist in the Haight-Ashbury is hung on a strange dilemma.")Leary, TimothyUndated130
Scope and Contents
Annotated in Leary's handLetter from James GoddardLeary, Timothy1966-04-05131
Scope and Contents
Autograph notes in pencil and ink, on verso of circular letter, unsigned, from James Goddard, Commissioner of Food and DrugsA group of photocopied legal contracts between Leary and Michel- Gustave Hauchard relating to the publishing and film rights of various Leary works1972212
Scope and Contents
Many with original ink annotations in Leary's handSeries II: Letters to and from Leary (and among his circle)1961-1988Typed letter signed, "G" to "Timothy &."Unknown1966-04-04132Photocopies of Correspondence with Allen GinsburgGinsburg, Allen1962-1963133Photocopies of Correspondence with Aldous HuxleyHuxley, Aldous1962-1963134Autograph Letter Signed to Michael HollengsheadLeary, Timothy1968-12-09135
Scope and Contents
Report on his happiness and optimism about his legal situation: "We too are enjoying Heaven…"Typed letter signed, "Tim" to "Attorney."Leary, Timothy1970136
Scope and Contents
With: Photocopy of "United States of America vs. Timothy Leary: Change of Counsel."Autograph Letter Signed "T' to Dr. Gene SchoenfeldLeary, Timothy1973-03-01137Autograph letter signed, "T" to "Michael"Leary, Timothy1973-03-01138
Scope and Contents
Leary thanks him for sending various publications and speculates about possible titles for his forthcoming book.Autograph letter signed, "James Peter Cavagnaro" to "Jan."Cavagnaro, James PeterUndated139
Scope and Contents
Writing from prison, he praises Leary's and Hesse's ideas on liberation and spiritual energy.Typed letter signed, "Walter" to "Betsy."Clark, Walter Houston1973-04-06140
Scope and Contents
Together with: Appeal Against Further Trial & Persecution of Dr. Timothy LearyTyped letter signed, "Arthur Kleps" to "Mr. Volkening."Kleps, Arthur.1964-06-08141Typed letter signed, "Paul" to "Tim or whoever."Krassner, Paul1964-11-20142
Scope and Contents
Krassner inquiring if Leary will be conducting a seminar at Millbrook in late NovemberTyped letter signed, "Paul" to "Tim, Dick, and Ralph."Krassner, Paul1964-09-27143
Scope and Contents
Krassner informing Leary that his new article was to be entitled "Dr. Leary – or How I Learned to Transcend Ego and Expand My Consciousness (Notes from a Found Weekend)."Typed note signed, "A.C. Spectorsky" to "Dr. Leary."Leary, Timothy1966144
Scope and Contents
Stapled to: Typed letter signed, from "Tim Leary" to "Mr.Spectorsky." With: Playboy Interview: Timothy Leary. Also with: Leary, Timothy. Typescript on Ira Cohen, LSD, and CannabisAutograph letter signed, "Alan" to "Tim."Watts, Alan1963-05-29145
Scope and Contents
Watts states that the both the FDA and Myron Stolaroff of the National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH) suspected the current liquid LSD in circulation had very serious impuritiesTyped letter signed in type, "Your Flower Peter" to "Dr. Timothy Leary."Orlovsky, Peter1961-01-05146
Scope and Contents
Orlovsky — Ginsberg's lover — writing Leary about his experiences with LSD.Autograph letter signed, "Ron" to "Tim"Thelin, RonUndated147Typed letters signed, "Art Kleps" to "Dr. Kissinger" and "Editor, Time Magazine."Kleps, Arthur1973-06-06-1973-04-25148
Scope and Contents
Kleps writes Kissinger in the midst of the Watergate crisis, and states the good that might come if the conspirators ended up in the same prison as LearyTyped letter signed, "T" to "Beloved Joanna"Leary, TimothyUndated149
Scope and Contents
A draft of Leary's "Signal From Galactic Transmission Center to Planet Earth," which presents as "call home" from extraterrestrial species to the human race.Typed letter signed, from "Richard Alpert" to "Dr. Hofmann"Alpert, Richard1963-02-01150
Scope and Contents
A letter written by Alpert on behalf of Leary, who was in Mexico at the time.Typed letter signed, to Dr. Humphry Osmond, with partial draftMetzner, Ralph151
Scope and Contents
Metzner disputes Osmond's critical positionson both the Psychedelic Review and Leary.Autograph note signed, "Arthur" to unknownKleps, ArthurUndated152
Scope and Contents
Describing his travel plansTyped letter signed, from "Ralph" to "Joel."Metzner, Ralph1964153

Metzger encloses (not present) several brochures about upcoming psychedelic seminars and workshops.Typed letter signed, from "Frances" to "Timothy."UnknownUndated154Autograph letter signed, "Dr. Rene Bouvier."Bouvier, ReneUndated155

On verso: Family Dog Presents Poster.Large brown mailing envelope, annotated and signed by Timothy LearyLeary, Timothy1988-08156Series III: Leary's circle1963-1971Typescript SpeechUnknown1963181

Invitation to a benefit for the Timothy Leary Defense FundNeo-American Church Catechism and Handbook BrochureKleps, ArthurUndated191Emended Castalia Foundation BrochureCastalia1964192
Scope and Contents
Together with: Card insert: "How to Use Your Head." Mass mailer for presentation by Leary and Alpert on Sunday, November 29, 1964, at Town Hall in New York. Leary has amended the brochure to eliminate both the Castalia Foundation and Richard Alpert's names throughout, and change the title to "Psychedelic Workshops Conducted by Timothy Leary, Ph.D. and Ralph Metzner, Ph.D."Psychedelic Sweatshirts Ordering Form MailerMillbrook1965193The Stoned and...The UnstonedKleps, ArthurUndated194Membership Application to "The Neo-American Church."Kleps, ArthurUndated195Neo-American Church BulletinsKleps, Arthur1965-12-1966-09196
Scope and Contents
Invitation to a public baseball tournament, as well as other psychedelic news.Selected Bibliography on the Safety of LSD-25Metzner, RalphUndated197
Scope and Contents
A small selection of books and articles supporting the safety of LSD, some with additional quotes and reviews.Castalia Foundation Lecture Announcement PostcardCastalia1965198

An announcement for a lecture by Leary and Richard Alpert.Program Flyer for Psychedelic ExplorationsCastalia1965199Psychedelic Religious Celebration #1 and #2League for Spiritual DiscoveryUndated1100

Program for a League event, with Leary acting as "Guide," Ralph Metzner as "Harry, the Imprisoned Intellectual," and others in other roles.Psychedelic Review Subscription MaterialsPsychedelic Review19631101
Scope and Contents
The Psychedelic Review with TLS, from Felix Morrow and Ralph Metzger as well as a Psychedelic Review Subscription Direct Mail Package.International Federation for Internal Freedom Membership PackageIFIF19631102
Scope and Contents
Including; Statement on LSD and Psilocybin; Application for Membership; I.F.I.F. Newsletters; Marketing Letter, Typed, to "Dear Friend" from Leary; Present Program: IFIF – Cambridge; New Statement of Purposes; Sample Letter Draft; Paradise Lost by Mexico LSD Colony; Page 3 of Mimeograph Letter; Note.Psychedelic SessionsPsychedelic Review1965-1966 Fall/Winter1103

A marketing brochure for a series of workshops led by Leary and MetzgerCastalia Foundation Experiential WorkshopsCastalia19651104

A marketing brochure for a series of weekend workshops led by Leary, Metzner, and Alpert at the Castalia Foundation in Millbrook.On "Alternative Realities," "Biverses," "Multiverses" and Occultism in GeneralKleps, ArthurUndated1105

Begins: "Art if not life, what? Life if not art, why?..."Draft of The LSD Issue: A New Look at the WorldBlock, Joel N.Undated27Manuscript Notes for The LSD Issue: A New Look at the WorldBlock, Joel N.Undated28The LSD Issue: A New Look at the WorldBlock, Joel N.Undated29
Scope and Contents
A typescript teleplay for video featuring Dana Farnsworth, Dr. Theodore Rothman, Dr. Roy Grinker, and various experimental subjects, among others. No record of it having been produced has been found. Hitchcock-Balding Productions, to which the teleplay is addressed, also produced several of Leary's live speaking events, and had been engaged by the League of Spiritual Discovery to handle their public business.Timothy Leary Defense Fund Declaration of PrincipleUndated210

Leary Defense FundCommittee for the Reform of Marihuana Laws Statement of PurposeUndated211

Scope and Contents
Together with: Card insert: "How to Use Your Head." Mass mailer for presentation by Leary and Alpert on Sunday, November 29, 1964, at Town Hall in New York. Scope and Contents
A typescript teleplay for video featuring Dana Farnsworth, Dr. Theodore Rothman, Dr. Roy Grinker, and various experimental subjects, among others. No record of it having been produced has been found. Hitchcock-Balding Productions, to which the teleplay is addressed, also produced several of Leary's live speaking events, and had been engaged by the League of Spiritual Discovery to handle their public business.Timothy Leary Defense Fund Declaration of PrincipleUndated210
